data.title: Alstom signed contract to supply 47 DMU trains for Mexican rail corridors, part of National Development Plan 2025-2030.
data.description: On 26 December 2025, Alstom, a global leader in smart and sustainable mobility, secured a contract with the Railway Transport Regulatory Agency (ARTF) to provide 47 DMU (Diesel Multiple Unit) passenger trains. The agreement includes 33 long-haul trains and 14 short-haul trains designated for the Mexico City–Querétaro–Irapuato and Saltillo–Monterrey–Nuevo Laredo corridors in Mexico. The decision was disclosed during a public gathering of the decentralized agency of the Ministry of Infrastructure, Communications and Transportation on December 15. This initiative aligns with the National Development Plan 2025-2030 and serves as a significant advancement for passenger rail transportation in Mexico, facilitating connectivity between vital areas in the central and northern regions of the country and contributing to the enhancement of passenger train services.

data.title: Analysis by economist Paul Krugman warns of a "K-shaped" recession under Trump, contrasting Biden's pro-worker recovery. Wage growth for the bottom 25% of earners outpaced the top 25% between 2021-2023 but reversed under the current administration, widening income inequality. Krugman challenges the belief that economic inequality is constant, pointing to data showing a "Great Compression" during the Biden years.
data.description: Economist Paul Krugman is cautioning that the United States has transitioned from an inclusive, pro-worker recovery in the Joe Biden era to a polarized "Trump freeze," resulting in a new K-shaped economy where the wealthy prosper while the working class lags behind. Krugman challenges the belief in economic inequality as a fixed aspect of American life, citing Atlanta Fed data indicating a noteworthy "Great Compression" during the Biden presidency. Wage growth for the lowest 25% of earners outpaced the top 25% between 2021 and 2023 due to a tight labor market favoring low-paid workers. However, this positive trend has reversed under the current administration, with the income gap widening since late 2024. High-income individuals, particularly those with investments in the stock market, are experiencing financial growth, while low-income workers are seeing stagnant wage increases. The detailed account can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Trust Wallet, owned by Binance's CZ, to cover $7 million in user losses from a security breach. Users urged to upgrade.
data.description: Trust Wallet, a popular cryptocurrency wallet linked to Binance founder Changpeng "CZ" Zhao, has disclosed a security breach in a specific version of its browser extension. The breach resulted in the theft of at least $7 million, prompting the company to announce full reimbursement for impacted users. The issue was first noted by blockchain investigators who observed suspicious activity on the Trust Wallet browser extension during the holiday season. Following the installation of version 2.68, numerous user accounts were emptied of funds. Trust Wallet has advised users to disable the compromised extension and upgrade to the latest version, 2.69, to ensure security. Mobile users and other extension versions were not affected by the breach. For more details, visit a corresponding news platform.
